openmeter/dedupe/memorydedupe.purpose: In-memory LRU-backed deduplication for CloudEvents, used as the no-dependency fallback when Redis is unavailable. Implements dedupe.Deduplicator using a fixed-size hashicorp/golang-lru cache keyed by dedupe.Item.Key() with nil values — never stores event payloads.

patterns.name: LRU keyed by item.Key() with nil values
patterns.description: All dedup checks use item.Key() (composite of Namespace+ID+Source) as the LRU map key, storing nil as the value. Never store event payload data.

patterns.name: Atomic check-and-set via ContainsOrAdd for IsUnique
patterns.description: IsUnique must use ContainsOrAdd (single LRU call) to avoid a check-then-set race under concurrent ingest goroutines. CheckUnique and Set are separate non-atomic operations.

patterns.name: CheckUniqueBatch pre-allocates both result sets
patterns.description: Always initialise UniqueItems and AlreadyProcessedItems with make(dedupe.ItemSet, len(items)) before ranging; never append to nil maps.
patterns.example: result := dedupe.CheckUniqueBatchResult{UniqueItems: make(dedupe.ItemSet, len(items)), AlreadyProcessedItems: make(dedupe.ItemSet, len(items))}

patterns.name: Implement all five Deduplicator methods
patterns.description: Must satisfy dedupe.Deduplicator: IsUnique, CheckUnique, Set, Close, and CheckUniqueBatch. Close() is a no-op but must exist.

key_file_guides.role: Single-file implementation; NewDeduplicator(size) is the only constructor. defaultSize=1024 is used when size<1.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Callers must choose a meaningful cache size — too small causes premature LRU eviction, causing previously-seen events to appear unique again (false-unique bug).

key_file_guides.role: Validates atomic IsUnique (second call returns false) and that CheckUnique+Set are separate read/write operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background() — acceptable in tests only; production code must propagate caller context.

anti_patterns: Storing event payload data in the LRU cache — only nil values allowed, keyed by item.Key()
Using separate Contains + Add calls instead of ContainsOrAdd in IsUnique — creates a TOCTOU race under concurrent ingest
Skipping Close() — must exist even as a no-op to satisfy dedupe.Deduplicator interface
Using context.Background() in production implementation code (tests only)
Constructing CheckUniqueBatchResult without pre-allocated maps — nil map assignment panics

decisions.rationale: Bounded memory footprint for long-running sink workers; evicted entries can cause duplicate events to slip through, which is acceptable for the in-memory fallback (Redis is preferred for production).
A single LRU call avoids a check-then-set race under concurrent ingest goroutines without introducing a mutex around every operation.

code_examples.scenario: Implement a new Deduplicator satisfying dedupe.Deduplicator

openmeter/dedupe/redisdedupe.purpose: Redis-backed deduplication for CloudEvents in the sink worker, implementing dedupe.Deduplicator with TTL-based expiry, pipelined batch writes, and three key-format modes (rawkey, keyhash, keyhash-migration) to support zero-downtime migration of Redis key encoding.

patterns.name: Mode-switch on every key operation
patterns.description: Every method (IsUnique, CheckUnique, Set, CheckUniqueBatch) must switch on d.Mode to select the Redis key format. Never assume a single format; adding a new method without the switch breaks migration mode.

patterns.name: SetArgs with NX + TTL for atomic set-if-not-exists
patterns.description: All uniqueness writes use redis.SetArgs{TTL: d.Expiration, Mode: "nx"}. Return redis.Nil means the key already existed (duplicate). An empty status string also signals duplicate.
patterns.example: status, err := d.Redis.SetArgs(ctx, key, "", redis.SetArgs{TTL: d.Expiration, Mode: "nx"}).Result()

patterns.name: Pipelined batch writes in Set()
patterns.description: Set() pipelines multiple SetArgs calls via d.Redis.Pipelined. Inspect per-command errors individually — redis.Nil on a pipeline command means the key pre-existed (not a fatal error).
patterns.example:
cmds, err := d.Redis.Pipelined(ctx, func(pipe redis.Pipeliner) error { ... })
for i, cmd := range cmds { if errors.Is(cmd.Err(), redis.Nil) { existingItems = append(existingItems, items[i]) } }

patterns.name: keyhash-migration checks both old and new key formats
patterns.description: During DedupeModeKeyHashMigration, IsUnique sets the new hashed key first, then checks if the old rawkey exists in Redis to avoid false-unique results during rolling deploys.
patterns.example: isUnique, err := d.setKey(ctx, keyHash); if isUnique { isSet, _ := d.Redis.Exists(ctx, item.Key()).Result(); return isSet == 0, nil }

patterns.name: nil guard on d.Redis before any Redis call
patterns.description: IsUnique guards d.Redis == nil and returns an error. Replicate this guard in any new method that calls d.Redis directly.
patterns.example: if d.Redis == nil { return false, errors.New("redis client not initialized") }

key_file_guides.role: Full Deduplicator implementation; DedupeMode type and its three constants are the API extension point for key-format migration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: keyhash-migration mode reads both rawkey and keyhash formats to prevent false-unique events during rolling deploy — do not simplify without coordinating a full cutover.

key_file_guides.role: Standalone hashing helper using xxh3.HashString128 + base64.RawURLEncoding to shrink Redis keys by ~57% vs raw composite keys.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing the hash algorithm or encoding invalidates all existing Redis keys — causes a flood of duplicate events to be treated as unique. Any change requires a new migration mode.

anti_patterns: Using SET without NX — allows overwriting existing keys and defeats deduplication semantics
Adding a new DedupeMode without updating every method's switch statement
Ignoring redis.Nil in pipeline results — it signals a pre-existing key, not a fatal error
Calling d.Redis methods without the nil guard (replicate the guard from IsUnique in every new method)
Changing GetKeyHash algorithm without adding a new migration mode and Redis key rotation plan

decisions.decision: Three-mode key format with explicit migration mode
decisions.rationale: Allows a rolling upgrade from rawkey to keyhash without a big-bang cutover; migration mode checks both formats so no events are incorrectly classified as unique during the transition window.

decisions.decision: xxh3-128 + base64url over raw composite strings
decisions.rationale: 22-char base64url keys use ~57% less Redis memory than the ~77-char raw composite key while maintaining collision probability ~1e-30 at 300M events/day.

decisions.decision: TTL-based expiry instead of unbounded growth
decisions.rationale: Ingest events are only deduplicated within a recent window; expired keys free memory automatically without a separate purge job.

code_examples.scenario: Check uniqueness in DedupeModeKeyHash mode and write atomically with TTL
